goal: Migrate from React Router v5 to v6

sessions.label: session-1
sessions.rationale: Foundational shim that downstream lanes import. Lands first so the per-feature migrations can rebase on a stable surface.
work_to_do: Create src/lib/router-compat/ with v5→v6 helpers (useHistory→useNavigate, Redirect→Navigate).
Re-export the helpers from src/lib/router-compat.ts so feature lanes import a single path.
Unit tests covering each helper signature against v6 expectations.
sessions.notes: Don't touch feature directories — those belong to sessions 2-4.

sessions.label: session-2
sessions.rationale: Auth has the simplest route tree; serves as the reference migration for the larger feature areas.
work_to_do: Replace Switch/Route with Routes/Route under src/features/auth/.
Swap useHistory for useNavigate via the session-1 compat shim.
Update co-located tests to render with the v6 MemoryRouter.

sessions.label: session-3
sessions.rationale: Dashboard mirrors the auth migration pattern. Independent feature directory keeps the merge surface clean.
work_to_do: Convert nested Switch trees to the v6 Routes + Outlet pattern.
Adopt the compat hooks from session-1 wherever useHistory/match appears.

sessions.label: session-4
sessions.rationale: Settings has the deepest nested routes; isolating it lets the migration land without blocking the simpler feature areas.
work_to_do: Refactor settings sub-routes (profile, billing, team) to v6 nested Routes.

sessions.label: session-5
sessions.scope: Strip remaining v5 imports and bump package.json
sessions.rationale: Last mile — entry point, root router, dependency bump. Runs after every feature migration lands so the bump can't break in-flight work.
work_to_do: Replace BrowserRouter usage in src/main.tsx with the v6 createBrowserRouter pattern.
Update src/router.tsx to the new RouterProvider model.
Remove react-router-dom@5 from package.json and pin v6.
sessions.notes: Hold this lane until 2-4 merge — package.json is the choke point.
